title: From kickoff to canvas: connecting Google Workspace and Figma
speakers: Addison Rodomista (UX Design Lead, Google ), Chloe Fagan-Tucker (Google Workspace, BD & Strategic Partnerships, Google)
key_points: The talk frames the core problem as the "toggle tax" / fragmentation: design conversations and decisions are scattered across meetings, docs, and chats, so Figma can't be the single source of truth and teams constantly need realignment.

Walked through a designer persona "Steve" using the Figma + Google Workspace integration: Figma links dropping into Docs become native smart chips with metadata and thumbnails; an interactive Figma canvas embeds directly into Google Meet so peers can comment and duplicate frames in real time, not just watch a video feed.

Post-meeting continuity: Meet's "take notes for me" auto-captures crit feedback into a transcript, and because Meet chat is powered by Google Chat, conversations persist as searchable threads; the Figma app in Chat pings users (e.g., on mobile) so they can reply to Figma comments away from their desk.
Scale numbers cited for Google Workspace: 3 billion users, 13 million customers, and over 6 billion app installs from its marketplace.

Pitched the Workspace MCP server as the way for partners and "citizen developers" to pull Workspace context (Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Chat, People) into apps via AI natural-language prompts instead of APIs, plus new universal search; currently in developer preview with early-access partners before GA.

quotes.text: crit stops being about spectation and starts being about this real-time collaboration
quotes.talk: From kickoff to canvas: connecting Google Workspace and Figma

quotes.text: you can basically go from designer to developer all with using the tools that we are launching with workspace to bring those ideas to life
quotes.talk: From kickoff to canvas: connecting Google Workspace and Figma
